entry.title: Aveniq convinces as pilot partner for new certification: SAP security operations
entry.content:
We appreciate the long-standing partnership with Aveniq, a trusted SAP-certified operations partner for more than ten years, with now four SAP-certified services. In discussions with Aveniq, they shared that security will be their key focus in the coming years. We were therefore delighted to have them as a pilot partner for the launch of our new security operations certification.

Before introducing a new certification, SAP selects partners to go through the certification process as pilots to ensure that the process runs smoothly and is optimally tailored to the subject area. With its extensive SAP security knowledge, Aveniq was ideally suited to be a pilot partner for the SAP security operations certification.

In an interview with Aveniq, we learn which services they offer in the area of SAP security for SAP Cloud customers. They also explain why they have defined security as a strategic focus area for themselves and the importance of this topic for their customers. They also outline why Aveniq decided to participate as a pilot partner for the new SAP Security Operations certification and what benefits they expect to gain from this certification.

 

May you want to start by telling us about your company and what services Aveniq is offering for SAP security to cloud customers?

Aveniq is a leading Swiss IT service provider with around 650 employees and is primarily active in the financial services, healthcare, industrial, utilities, public sector, and education sectors. Aveniq offers its customers innovative services for a modern workplace, hybrid/cloud solutions, SAP consulting and implementation, hyper-automation options, digitalization solutions as well as CAD/PLM solutions, and consulting services with a focus on IT infrastructure, IT security, and business continuity.

Aveniq offers SAP Cloud customers a wide range of security services:

The services encompass a wide range of security-focused solutions, including the management of identity and access, covering users, roles, catalogs, single sign-on (SSO), provisioning, access control, and identity and access governance. They also involve checking and monitoring the security of SAP systems, ensuring the security of the SAP Cloud Connector, and supporting both internal and external audits through preparation and implementation. Furthermore, the offerings include training and raising awareness about security topics, analyzing code vulnerabilities in SAP applications, and implementing measures to reduce the attack surface at both the application and system levels.

The services also involve data protection and security, ensuring privacy and maintaining data integrity. They also address the remediation of security findings identified through the Early Watch Alert (EWA). Additionally, consulting services are offered to support the implementation and optimization of SAP security strategies, encompassing the analysis of security requirements, the development of tailored security concepts, and assistance throughout the implementation process.

 

What was the deciding factor for Aveniq to focus on security? And why is the topic so important for Aveniq and its customers? 

The importance of information security for long-term success and sustainability in a constantly changing, digital, and interconnected business world has increased massively. In the face of increasing cyber-attacks, a proactive, strategic approach to information security and data protection is essential. Aveniq has developed an end-to-end security strategy based on market needs and modern principles, encompassing both physical and digital security, management security, and operational security.

The topic of security is particularly important for several reasons:

Holistic approach: Aveniq emphasizes the importance of making no distinction between IT and OT, and taking a comprehensive approach that implements security, privacy, and protection across all levels according to the principle of “Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability” as per NIST CSF 2.0 and ZeroTrust. This includes systems, processes, products, information/data, people, and suppliers, i.e. the protection of company assets.

Improved security: Comprehensive, preventive security measures ensure a high level of protection that covers all attack vectors. This is supported by continuous monitoring and rapid response measures (response, recovery), which are ensured by our Security Operations Center (SOC).

Building trust: Compliance with international security standards and data protection principles, evidenced by various certifications such as ISO 27001, ISO 22301, and others, strengthens trust.

Adaptability: Flexibility and control in the hybrid and multi-cloud model enable specific customer needs to be met efficiently.

Increased efficiency: Consolidation of technologies and processes leads to improved infrastructure and application management and optimized costs for customers.

Customer-centric solutions: Modular and replicable services are designed to meet customers' individual requirements and business objectives, considering regulatory requirements and risk appetite.

This security orientation helps to strengthen the competitive position of customers and enables Aveniq to further expand its market position.

 

What factors influenced Aveniq's decision to support SAP as a pilot partner in the validation of the new certification 'SAP security operations'?

Since its initial certification as an SAP operations partner in 2011, Aveniq has continued to develop and adapt to changing customer needs and SAP's strategic direction. With a broad range of services - from SAP HANA operations services and various operating models such as RISE with SAP services to SAP remote support and SAP hosting in the Aveniq Cloud and via hyperscalers - we have been able to establish ourselves in the Swiss market with around 100 internal SAP specialists.

In 2018, we were one of the first partners in Switzerland to demonstrate our expertise in SAP S/4HANA solutions operations with a certification. Our certification for SAP BTP operations in 2024 illustrates our ongoing innovation and commitment to providing customers with advanced and secure SAP solutions. Our comprehensive knowledge of SAP security enables us to make a decisive contribution to the security and efficiency of our customers' SAP systems. Certification for SAP security operations was therefore the next logical step to put our expertise in this area to the test.

The fact that SAP has chosen us as a pilot partner for the new certification fills us with pride and gratitude. The joint pursuit of excellence is the cornerstone of our long-standing partnership as equals. We are delighted that the auditors were impressed by the quality of our SAP security services.

 

What benefits do you anticipate from being SAP-certified in SAP security operations for SAP cloud solutions?

We see several advantages in SAP certification for SAP security operations for SAP cloud solutions. 

The certification strengthens the customers’ confidence in Aveniq's security expertise and underlines the company's credibility as a trusted partner for SAP security solutions. It ensures that Aveniq's SAP security operation services meet the highest standards and are continuously improved. This allows Aveniq to provide its customers with a higher level of protection and improved service quality, leading to increased customer satisfaction. Furthermore, the certification strengthens the long-standing partnership between SAP and Aveniq, fostering mutual benefits for both parties.

In summary, SAP certification not only strengthens a company's trust and credibility but also helps to optimize internal processes and open up new markets. It provides clear proof of compliance with standards and quality requirements, which is of great value to existing and potential customers as well as to the manufacturer itself.

 

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com

entry.title: D/C-Code does not fit to sign of amount in some items
entry.content:
For API posting error

Type ID: 047(FINS_FI_POSTING_IF) 

Note D/C-Code does not fit to sign of amount in some items Type ID

is raised


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: D/C-Code does not fit to sign of amount in some items
entry.content:
See help documentation Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous)

..

"This service supports negative posting on header level. Negative posting can be set if DebitCreditCode doesn't sign off the amount in all items. If some items are signed off and others not, an error message is issued."

See also referenced in Journal Entry


The service Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous) (JournalEntryCreateRequestConfirmation_In) supports negative posting on header level. You must fill in the DebitCreditCode for all items. If you do this partially, an error message is issued.

If the amount is positive, you must set DebitCreditCode to 'H'. If the amount is negative, you must set it to 'S', as illustrated in the sample codes below. The item is then marked as negative posting.

See Blog - Guidelines for API Journal Entry – Post, the following point is made on how to create negative postings via API: 

5.9 Consulting about Negative Posting

The JE Post API supports negative posting on header level. To enable negative posting, you must fill in the DebitCreditCode for all items. If the amount is positive, you must set DebitCreditCode to 'H'. If the amount is negative, you must set it to 'S', as illustrated in the sample codes below. The item is then marked as negative posting.

If you do this partially, an error message ‘D/C-Code does not fit to sign of amount in some items’ is issued.

See also kba 3464123 Troubleshooting APIs for General Ledger Accounting


Ensure you fully test on your test system prior to implementation on production to ensure no negative impact.

entry.title: Re: Character are doubled when exporting Report to PDF wi...
entry.content:
Hi 

To resolve this issues , please check the below points 

Ensure the problematic font (e.g., Ubuntu) is properly installed on the system hosting CR2020.
Check the "Export PDF" settings in CR to enable font embedding.
Modify the registry settings: add or adjust the ForceLargerFonts or PdfExportOptions parameters under HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\SAP BusinessObjects\Crystal Reports.
Use a more compatible font if issues persist.
Test PDF export via the .NET runtime for better results, as it may handle fonts differently.

Ensure all software is updated to the latest service pack.

entry.title: Partial Quantity Billing for Billing Plans
entry.content:
Hello,

We have one business Requirement as explained below

For example ,

A Sales Order will be created for a quantity of 10, including a billing plan. The billing plan consists of two components: the first is a 10% down payment, and the second is the final (closing) invoice. For the first billing plan, a down payment request will be generated for the full quantity of 10. Once the down payment request is cleared, delivery will take place. During this delivery, we delivered a quantity of 8 to the customer. According to the standard configuration, the system defaults to copying the full quantity of 10 when generating the invoice. However, we require the invoice to be created for the delivered quantity of 8 instead

I understand that this may not be possible in the standard configuration, but I would greatly appreciate it if anyone knows of an alternative solution.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content:
Hello @Julh 

If both storage locations are linked to the same warehouse number, which I assume is the case, then the MM movement type 311 is mapped to WM movement type 309. Check the configuration of the WM movement 309 and verify "post to same bin" checkbox:

If it is checked, posting changes are processed without changing the bin.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @FatemaPatel,

to get certified you need to use a certification attempt ( 4 attempts are included in the SAP Learning Hub subscription) and at least one attend is needed to pass the exam-when you pass it the first time.

When you then are certified you can stay certified via passing an assessment  - at minimum every 12 month. To access those assessments, you also need to have a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription.

The available versions of SAP Learning Hub are listed here: SAP Learning Hub | Your Online E-Learning Enablement Platform - either you can purchase the customer version or your company is an SAP Partner and has purchased the partner edition for you already. 

Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Re: Groovy script to update incoming xml payload element
entry.content:
Hi 

To achieve the required output where specific XML element values are replaced with new ones,
you can use the following Groovy script in integration platforms like SAP CPI or other similar environments:

import groovy.xml.XmlUtil

// Input payload
def inputPayload = '''<EDI_DC40>
<TABNAM>ORDERSHIPPED</TABNAM>
<MANDT>100</MANDT>
<DOCNUM></DOCNUM>
<DOCREL>640</DOCREL>
<STATUS>5</STATUS>
<DIRECT>2</DIRECT>
<IDOCTYP>DELVRY03</IDOCTYP>
<CIMTYP/>
<MESTYP>YT_LA_WHSCON</MESTYP>
<SNDPOR>LS</SNDPOR>
<SNDPRT>DELVRY03</SNDPRT>
<SNDPRN>LS</SNDPRN>
<RCVPOR></RCVPOR>
<RCVPRT>DELVRY03</RCVPRT>
<RCVPRN>P77</RCVPRN>
<CREDAT>20240830</CREDAT>
<CRETIM>121540</CRETIM>
</EDI_DC40>'''

// Parse the XML
def xml = new XmlParser().parseText(inputPayload)

// Replace values
xml.SNDPRN[0].value = 'BTP_IS'
xml.RCVPRN[0].value = 'ZEUABC'

// Convert back to string
def outputPayload = XmlUtil.serialize(xml)

// Return updated payload
return outputPayload

Output:

The script will produce the desired XML:
<EDI_DC40>
<TABNAM>ORDERSHIPPED</TABNAM>
<MANDT>100</MANDT>
<DOCNUM/>
<DOCREL>640</DOCREL>
<STATUS>5</STATUS>
<DIRECT>2</DIRECT>
<IDOCTYP>DELVRY03</IDOCTYP>
<CIMTYP/>
<MESTYP>YT_LA_WHSCON</MESTYP>
<SNDPOR>LS</SNDPOR>
<SNDPRT>DELVRY03</SNDPRT>
<SNDPRN>BTP_IS</SNDPRN>
<RCVPOR/>
<RCVPRT>DELVRY03</RCVPRT>
<RCVPRN>ZEUABC</RCVPRN>
<CREDAT>20240830</CREDAT>
<CRETIM>121540</CRETIM>
</EDI_DC40>


Note:

Adjust headers/properties dynamically if required by fetching values from variables or runtime configurations. Ensure the integration tool supports Groovy scripting.

entry.title: Re: Feature Request: Make table headers sticky in Signavi...
entry.content:
Hi 

Currently, SAP Signavio Process Intelligence does not support sticky headers in tables. To improve usability, you can submit a feature request to SAP via the Influence portal or your support contact. As a workaround, export the data to Excel or similar tools where you can freeze headers for better readability. Alternatively, embed the table in an HTML page using CSS with `position: sticky` for headers. For immediate support, check the SAP Signavio community for potential custom solutions.

Temporary Workarounds

If embedding the table in an external webpage is an option (e.g., for sharing dashboards):

Use HTML with CSS to achieve sticky headers:

<style>
table {
border-collapse: collapse;
width: 100%;
}
th {
position: sticky;
top: 0;
background-color: #f2f2f2;
z-index: 1;
}
</style>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Column 1</th>
<th>Column 2</th>
<th>Column 3</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Data 1</td>
<td>Data 2</td>
<td>Data 3</td>
</tr>
<!-- More rows -->
</tbody>
</table>

entry.title: Need Help with Multi-Level Item Grouping in SAP Customer Checkout
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m facing a challenge in SAP Customer Checkout and would appreciate your guidance. I need to create item groupings with up to five levels of depth, where the final level represents the specific item for sale. For example:

  • Cake
    • Round Cake
      • Round Chocolate Cake
        • Round Chocolate Cake (Chilled)
          • Final Item

At each level, the system should allow me to drill down to the next grouping until I reach the final item for sale.

Does anyone know how to configure SAP Customer Checkout to achieve this? Are there specific features, configurations, or extensions I should explore?

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAP Api Gateway - unauthorized... in PL language
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm having a problem generating a CR through the API Gateway.

Here are my steps in Postman:

  1. Login to ulr-api-gateway/login with the following body:

 

{
    "UserName": "UserName",
    "Password": "Password",
    "CompanyDB": "DB_NAME",
    "DBInstance": "DBInstance"
}
I received a SESSION token
2. Call to ulr-api-gateway/rs/v1/LoadCR?DocCode=XXX - executed successfully.
Result:
{
    "error": false,
    "resultSet": [
        {
            "parameterType": "StoredProcedureParameter",
            "values": [],
            "defaultValuesDescription": {},
.....
3. Call to ulr-api-gateway/rs/v1/ExportPDFData?DocCode=XXX.
I received a 200 status code, but the result body was:
"Nie masz uprawnień do uruchomienia tego raportu; skontaktuj się z administratorem, aby zaktualizować ustawienia w sekcji "Uprawnienia""
 
The user account I used to log into SAP has all necessary permissions
Administration => System Initialization => Authorizations => General Authorizations => Interface Api
and the "admin" permissions in account section

entry.title: Re: S4 Upgrade: Starting the Shadow Instance after a reboot
entry.content:
Good Morning,

As you proposed, I also checked the dev_w0 that showed another problem:
C no connection data found for key (UPGSHDKEY) in HANA secure store
C *** ERROR => Connect to database failed, rc=-10909, rcSQL=-10104

The User Store UPGSHDKEY was missing, I recreated

# su - xxxadm
% hdbuserstore -i set UPGSHDKEY xxxxx:30013@XXX SAPHANADBSHD
% hdbsql -U UPGSHDKEY
Welcome to the SAP HANA Database interactive terminal.
Type: \h for help with commands
\q to quit
hdbsql XXX=>

After that the shadow could start 🙂

Have a nice day.

entry.title: Re: Newly added fields not displaying in table maintainance generator
entry.content:
Hi, I tried and found a solution

When you table is edited activate it.

While opening the table table from se11. Choose change button

Select Utilities (in Menu bar) -> Table Maintenance Generator.

Click edit button with pencil. Select all checkboxes. and continue. This will regenerate the complete maintenance generator

entry.title: Re: Add delay in Request message SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi 

To achieve this in SAP PO, you can use the following approach:
Define a Dummy Receiver: Create a dummy receiver channel that accepts messages but does not forward them to the S4 system. This will ensure the 3rd-party sender doesn't receive a response while preventing HTTP errors like 500 or 404.
Use a Delay Mechanism: Implement a UDF (User-Defined Function) or Java mapping to introduce a delay in processing. Alternatively, use a BPM/Integration Process in SAP PO to hold messages in a "wait" state for a defined time before processing.
Audit Logs for Monitoring: Ensure the interface is set to log all messages for visibility in SAP PO monitoring.
Route to S4 After Delay: Once the delay period is over, route the message to the S4 receiver channel for further processing.

entry.title: ME52N closed document going in editable mode BAPI_REQUISITION_DELETE
entry.content:
Hi gurus, The query is why the user is able to make changes once the PR is in closed status ?
User is expecting to get the delete indicator and closed indicator to be checked for the PR
created 45 days.

There is custom program and BAPI_REQUISITION_DELETE is used to delete and close the items.
The following is the code ::

 data lv_date1 type datum.
  data lv_date2 type datum.

  call function 'RP_CALC_DATE_IN_INTERVAL'
    exporting
      date      = sy-datum
      days      = '46'
      months    = '00'
      signum    = '-'
      years     = '00'
    importing
      calc_date = lv_date1.

  call function 'RP_CALC_DATE_IN_INTERVAL'
    exporting
      date      = sy-datum
      days      = '00'
      months    = '06'
      signum    = '-'
      years     = '00'
    importing
      calc_date = lv_date2.


  if lv_date1 is not initial.
    select banfn, bnfpo from eban
      into table @DATA(lt_eban)
      where badat le @LV_date1
      and   frgkz IN ('C', 'D')
      and loekz NE 'X'.
  endif.

*  if lv_date2 is not initial.
*    select banfn, bnfpo from eban
*      appending table @LT_eban
*      where badat le @LV_date2
*      and   frgkz = 'A'
*      and   statu = 'N'.
*  endif.


  if lt_eban is not initial.

    data lt_items type table of bapieband.
    data ls_items type bapieband.
    data(lt_head) = lt_eban[].
    data lt_ret type table of bapireturn.
    data ls_log type zmm_pr_del_log.

    sort lt_head by banfn.
    delete adjacent duplicates from lt_head comparing all fields.

    loop at lt_head into data(ls_head).
      refresh lt_items[].
      refresh lt_ret[].

      loop at lt_eban into data(ls_eban) where banfn = ls_head-banfn.
        ls_items-preq_item = ls_eban-bnfpo.
        ls_items-delete_ind = 'X'.
        ls_items-closed     = 'X'.    "PASHA 17/10/2024
        append ls_items to lt_items.
        clear ls_items.
      endloop.

      call function 'BAPI_REQUISITION_DELETE'
        exporting
          number                      = ls_head-banfn
        tables
          requisition_items_to_delete = lt_items
          return                      = lt_ret.

      read table lt_ret into data(ls_ret) with key type = 'E'.
      if sy-subrc = 0.
        ls_log-banfn = ls_head-banfn.
        loop at lt_items into ls_items.
          ls_log-bnfpo = ls_items-preq_item.
          ls_log-message = ls_ret-message.
          ls_log-zdate = sy-datum.
          ls_log-uzeit = sy-uzeit.
          ls_log-uname = sy-uname.
          ls_log-status = 'E'.
          modify zmm_pr_del_log from ls_log.
        endloop.
        clear ls_log.
      else.
        ls_log-banfn = ls_head-banfn.
        loop at lt_items into ls_items.
          ls_log-bnfpo = ls_items-preq_item.
          ls_log-message = ls_ret-message.
          ls_log-zdate = sy-datum.
          ls_log-uzeit = sy-uzeit.
          ls_log-uname = sy-uname.
          ls_log-status = 'S'.
          modify zmm_pr_del_log from ls_log.
        endloop.
        clear ls_log.
      endif.


    endloop.

  endif.

I have observed two scenarios at me52n once this code gets executed.

for EBAN-ESTKG (PRODUCTION ORDER(F)) it is giving me the expected result which is not editable after
running the above code.

However ,

For all other scenarios other than production order it is going in editable mode after running through the same above code like below

Strange thing is that in the first scenario closed is not ticked but it was non editable , in the second one closed is ticked but i can able to undelete and make changes.


My question is why it is working fine in PRODUCTION ORDER case but not in other cases though
the code is same.

The requirement is once the code is executed it should be non editable for all documents based on the select query in the code.

Is it that needs to be fixed from functional end or is it SAP's glitch as it is making
item number editable after the PR is closed.

Thank in advance for your replies

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Replication Flow (Delta Functionality)
entry.content:
Hello @DanielIngenhaag

I've 2 questions about DELTA replications:

1. While replicating data, noticed that SUCCESS file is getting generated only for INITIAL replications, whereas it's not the case with DELTA replications. We need SUCCESS file after each successful DELTA replication to:

  • As confirmation that replication is completed successfully.
  • Use it as Trigger for further processing the Replicated file.

2. My 2nd query is about generating message for DELTA replication, in case there is any failure. As We can't include Delta into taskchain, is there any provision which can send Notification email in case Delta replication had failed.

Lastly, Expected timeframe for DELTA replications inclusion in Taskchain. Hopefully that will take care of lots of Adhoc and Dependency based Delta replication. Timing right an interval-based Delta replication is almost impossible for these scenarios.

Thanks,

Deepak Jain

In your experience with SAP, have you noticed how your unique traits influence your workflow, learning style, or the way you tackle challenges? I'd love to hear how personality impacts the way you use SAP tools and collaborate within teams!

entry.title: Recap: ICPM 2024
entry.content:
Overview

This year, over 400 researchers, vendors, and practitioners from around the globe gathered in Copenhagen, Denmark, for the sixth International Conference on Process Mining (ICPM). Held from October 14th to 18th, 2024, the conference offered five days of diverse sessions, including research presentations and demonstrations, as well as an industry-focused track featuring vendor-led tutorials, use-case discussions, and a hackathon. With this duality, ICPM uniquely bridges academia and industry, making it an exciting event for the process mining community—and a key conference for SAP Signavio. In this blog post, discover how our colleagues represented SAP Signavio across various sessions, explore the hot topics highlighted at the conference, and celebrate our accomplishment in winning the Best Paper Award.

SAP Signavio at ICPM

SAP Signavio took an active role on both sides of the conference: research and industry. As a platinum sponsor, SAP Signavio’s booth served as a lively hub of discussion among colleagues, partners, and attendees. Gia-Thi Nguyen and Gregor Berg presented how SAP Signavio, LeanIX, and WalkMe support business transformation initiatives. Meanwhile, various colleagues from Signavio’s innovation unit, Signavio.NEXT, contributed research to the conference. Led by and in collaboration with Timotheus Kampik, the team—including master's and PhD students, visiting researchers, and engineers—presented papers on numerous hot topics.

Hot Topics

ICPM featured a range of hot topics, with generative AI (GenAI) continuing to make waves in the field. However, one particularly prominent topic this year was simulation, generating even more buzz throughout the conference. ICPM is a forum for presenting both foundational research and the newest techniques in process mining, making it a stage also for the latest advancements in GenAI. The conference offered a new GenAI workshop to highlight the latest applications, with a main-track presentation from our Signavian Adrian Rebmann exploring the current capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in process mining. While GenAI was a key theme, simulation was the highlight, getting more and more traction in recent years. The main keynote provided a comprehensive overview of different simulation approaches, discussing both differences and commonalities among them. Additionally, several papers proposed new data-driven simulation algorithms.

Best Paper Award: AgentSimulator

One such simulation approach was developed in a research project by our Signavians Lukas Kirchdorfer, Robert Blümel, and Timotheus Kampik, in collaboration with professors Han van der Aa and Heiner Stuckenschmidt. We are incredibly proud to receive the ICPM Best Paper Award, highlighting the impact and recognition of SAP Signavio in the academic community. The paper introduces a novel approach for business process simulation using agent-based modeling. While traditional simulation approaches focus on the global control-flow perspective of the process, AgentSimulator models processes by simulating individual agents representing resources, each with unique characteristics and decision-making capabilities. The strength of this approach lies in its ability to better capture the complexity of real-world processes, especially those involving human participants, where variability and autonomy play significant roles. Therefore, it allows for potentially more realistic simulations, providing valuable insights into how processes change in different scenarios.

Conclusion

ICPM 2024 showcased several emerging trends transforming the process mining field. Topics ranged from agent-based simulation models to the use of GenAI for providing contextual explanations, offering a forward-looking perspective on how businesses can harness data to gain deeper insights and enhance their processes. For those interested in learning about the details of the latest process mining advancements, the full range of papers is publicly available. Also, feel free to get in touch with the Blue Sky Team at Signavio and join our weekly discussions about the latest research in process mining.

entry.title: Re: Remove Review content button from Learning History fo...
entry.content:
@JoClarkson, You should be able to disable the setting "Users can Review Completed Content from History" on the item's Online Content tab. To find that setting, click the gear icon on the right above the list of content modules) to open the Online Content Settings page, and then click the Completion menu. Toggle the setting off, click OK to close the window, and then click Save on the content module page. Since you are not making changes to the content module structure, you should not be prompted to "reassign" the changes to users who have the item assigned but who are incomplete. If you find you are not able to save the changes, check the LMS_ADMIN configuration file to see if lockItemContentStructure is enabled.

If your company currently manages item revisions manually by an admin, then you might consider asking the admins to make this change on the superseded revision after they have created the new revision. However, I understand the manual burden this change would represent, particularly if your company has a large number of revision-controlled procedures. To address this issue at our company, we chose to disable the "Review Completed Content from History" setting when revising procedure items that are linked to content on our document management server.

However, if your company manages item revisions using the item connector, you can add the "REVIEWABLE" field to the item data connector header and map your data to send a value of N to disable this setting for specific item revisions. You could choose to set the field to N for just superseded revisions that are being inactivated or use it to set the field to N for new revisions also. Some caveats: If your company is not yet using the item connector to manage revisions, you should seek the assistance of an implementation partner as the original implementation can be tricky. If you company is currently using the connector as part of an item revision integration with your document management system, adding a new field to the connector header will require updates to the tool that populates the connector. But even if your company does not want to use the item connector as part of an item revision integration, you could use it as an item bulk import tool that you manually populate and execute when needed. Very handy when you have large item clean-up projects!

Good luck!
Mary Katherine
